evidence: Main window geometry is captured from Tauri physical position/size and persisted to main-window-state.json under app data without scale-factor normalization.
Startup keeps the main window hidden until saved main window size/position restore or cursor-display fallback placement completes, then explicitly shows/focuses the window.
Saved main window size/position restores with Tauri PhysicalSize/PhysicalPosition before falling back to cursor-display placement, and reasserts always-on-top after restore.
Restore rejects saved geometry that no longer intersects any current monitor work area, preventing stale off-screen placement after display topology changes.
State writes go through a same-directory temporary file before rename so successful writes replace stale temp output instead of directly truncating the persisted state.

Windows builds unlink an existing persisted state file before renaming the temporary state file, avoiding stale geometry after the first save on platforms where rename does not replace existing files.

Rust unit tests cover JSON persistence, malformed JSON fallback, app-data state path construction, physical geometry preservation for mixed-DPI restore, stale temporary state replacement, Windows-style existing-target replacement policy, and off-screen geometry rejection.
remainingEvidence: Runtime relaunch screenshot and terminal output confirming restored geometry were not captured in this CLI run.
The persisted transparency field is recorded in state JSON, but visual transparency relaunch evidence remains pending.

evidence: Channel server startup binds to 0.0.0.0 by default on a dynamic OS-selected port outside the reserved 3100..=3199 range.
ChannelServerState records LAN host candidates for QR/config consumers.
cargo test --manifest-path apps/stage-tauri/Cargo.toml channel_server verifies the async TCP server returns HTTP 200 JSON on /health.
remainingEvidence: A second-machine curl to http://<LAN-IP>:<port>/health was not captured in this CLI run.

evidence: Tray menu setup adds stable Show AIRI and Quit entries using Tauri tray/menu APIs.
Tray Quit, electron_app_quit, and main-window CloseRequested now route through quit_after_persisting, which persists state and calls app.exit(0).
Rust unit tests cover stable tray menu IDs and tray menu action routing.
remainingEvidence: Runtime ps output showing no cargo/rustc/airi-tauri orphan processes after tray quit and window close was not captured in this CLI run.

evidence: Tauri setup manages ChannelServerState and spawns start_channel_server with tauri::async_runtime::spawn.
The channel server uses tokio::net::TcpListener on a dynamic non-reserved port and serves GET/HEAD /health.

cargo test --manifest-path apps/stage-tauri/Cargo.toml channel_server covers reserved port detection, URL formatting, health JSON, HTTP status handling, shared state snapshots, accept-error continuation, split request-line reads, and async TCP /health requests returning HTTP 200 JSON.
cargo test --manifest-path apps/stage-tauri/Cargo.toml passed with 43 Rust tests.
remainingEvidence: Runtime curl -i http://localhost:<port>/health from a launched desktop app was not captured in this CLI run.

evidence: Rust tests verify electron:server-channel:get-qr-payload serializes the shared QR payload contract and errors until the channel-server port is known.
Rust tests verify QR candidate URLs use ws://, /ws, LAN-host preference, and IPv6 bracket formatting.
Tauri Eventa tests verify the server-channel QR invoke maps to electron_server_channel_get_qr_payload and is exported from @proj-airi/tauri-eventa root.
Stage Tauri Vitest tests verify QR payload JSON serialization, SVG data URL generation, unavailable-state handling, refresh error state, and /settings/connection route selection.
Stage Tauri renderer now mounts ServerChannelQrCard when windowRoute.kind is settings-connection.
Stage Tauri Vite config now resolves Cubism SDK from a persistent AIRI Live2D cache, validates Core/live2dcubismcore.min.js, and stages it into .cache/public without re-downloading on cache hit.
A fake-cache Vite startup check reached VITE ready on port 1437 without the Cubism download message.
remainingEvidence: Runtime screenshot of the Tauri settings connection QR card remains pending; the previous cold-worktree Cubism download blocker is addressed by facd71ac6, but a full cargo tauri dev screenshot was not captured in this CLI run.
pnpm -F @proj-airi/stage-tauri typecheck and build remain blocked by pre-existing @proj-airi/electron-screen-capture type resolution through packages/stage-shared/src/beat-sync/detector.ts.

evidence: Managed window specs and route tests cover settings, chat, widgets, caption, notice, about, onboarding, devtools, beat-sync, inlay, and dashboard.
stage-tauri build, route Vitest, tauri-eventa adapter tests, typechecks, Cargo tests/build, and whitespace checks passed.
Codex reviewer found notice native-close and widget ttlMs gaps; both were fixed and follow-up review reported no remaining issues.

evidence: apps/stage-tauri/tauri.conf.json already sets the main window alwaysOnTop:true.
apps/stage-tauri/src/window_manager.rs now reapplies set_always_on_top(true) during Tauri setup.
cargo test, cargo build, cargo fmt --check, git diff --check, and stage-tauri typecheck passed.

evidence: Main window startup now centers the floating window on the monitor under the cursor using Tauri monitor_from_point/cursor_position.
Managed windows without explicit coordinates now center on the main/current monitor instead of Tauri global center().
screen get-all-displays/get-primary-display/get-cursor-screen-point now use Tauri monitor/cursor APIs instead of a fake primary display.
Rust unit tests cover display-centered placement, explicit-coordinate precedence, oversized-window origin clamping, and physical-to-logical monitor conversion.
